---
title: "Output & Entity Utils"
description: "PredictionWriter putative group output and TimeEntity utilities"
---

## PredictionWriter

`PredictionWriter` (`output/PredictionWriter.hpp`) writes ML outputs to `DataManager` and the entity system. It implements the **putative group pattern**: predictions never overwrite user-created labels — they are written to separate groups and data keys with a configurable prefix.

### Output Formats

| Output | Format | Description |
|---|---|---|
| **Intervals** | `DigitalIntervalSeries` | Contiguous runs of each predicted class, keyed as `"{prefix}{class_name}"` |
| **Probabilities** | `AnalogTimeSeries` | Per-class probability values (one series per class), keyed as `"{prefix}prob_{class_name}"` |
| **Entity groups** | `EntityGroupManager` | TimeEntities grouped by predicted class, following putative group naming |

### Configuration

```cpp
struct PredictionWriterConfig {
    std::string output_prefix = "Predicted:";
    bool write_intervals = true;
    bool write_probabilities = true;
    bool write_to_putative_groups = true;
    std::string time_key_str = "time";
};
```

### Result

```cpp
struct PredictionWriterResult {
    std::vector<std::string> interval_keys;   // Created interval series keys
    std::vector<std::string> probability_keys; // Created probability series keys
    std::vector<GroupId> putative_group_ids;    // Created entity group IDs
};
```

### Usage

The pipelines call `PredictionWriter` as their final step. It can also be used directly:

```cpp
PredictionWriterConfig config;
config.output_prefix = "Predicted:";

auto result = writePredictions(
    dm, predictions, probabilities, class_names,
    valid_row_indices, config);
```

## Classification Metrics

`ClassificationMetrics` (`metrics/ClassificationMetrics.hpp`) computes standard metrics after prediction.

### Binary Metrics

```cpp
struct BinaryClassificationMetrics {
    double accuracy;
    double sensitivity;   // true positive rate
    double specificity;   // true negative rate
    double dice;          // F1 score
    ConfusionMatrix confusion_matrix;
};
```

### Multi-Class Metrics

```cpp
struct MultiClassMetrics {
    double overall_accuracy;
    std::vector<PerClassMetrics> per_class; // precision, recall, F1 per class
    ConfusionMatrix confusion_matrix;
};
```

Both include `toString()` formatting utilities for human-readable output.

## Class Balancing

`ClassBalancing` (`preprocessing/ClassBalancing.hpp`) addresses class imbalance before training.

### Strategies

| Strategy | Behavior |
|---|---|
| `Subsample` | Down-sample majority class to `minority_count × max_ratio` |
| `Oversample` | Up-sample minority class to match majority |

### Configuration

```cpp
struct BalancingConfig {
    BalancingStrategy strategy = BalancingStrategy::Subsample;
    double max_ratio = 1.0;   // majority-to-minority ratio
    std::optional<std::size_t> seed; // for reproducibility
};
```

The `BalancedData` result includes `original_indices` for traceability back to the unbalanced dataset.

## TimeEntity Utilities

`TimeEntityUtils` (`entity_utils/TimeEntityUtils.hpp`) provides bulk operations for the `TimeEntity` kind in the entity system.

### Functions

| Function | Description |
|---|---|
| `registerTimeEntities(registry, frames, time_key)` | Bulk-register frames as TimeEntity IDs |
| `intervalsToTimeEntities(registry, intervals, time_key)` | Expand interval series to per-frame entities |
| `timeEntitiesToIntervals(group_manager, group_id, time_key)` | Convert entity groups back to interval series |

These utilities bridge the entity system with the ML pipeline's need to create per-frame entity groups for prediction output.
